descriptions: Bandidos ambushed Wino's Crew members.
edges.evidence_quote: Around twenty Bandidos members ambushed members of three rival clubs – the Cossacks, Ghost Riders and Wino's Crew – at a bar in Fort Worth on December 12, 2014

descriptions: The Vice Lords consolidated the Commanches into their nation.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang was successfully consolidating smaller neighborhood gangs (including the Cherokees, the Morphines, the Commanches, the Continental Pimps, the Imperial Chaplains, the Clovers, the Cobras, and the Braves) into the Vice Lord Nation.

descriptions: The Brindisi feud targeted major SCU members and was led by the Buccarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: capeggiata allora dal clan Buccarella ( Tuturano ) .

descriptions: Hog Riders MC is a support club for Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Three members of the Hog Riders Motorcycle Club, a support club for the Hells Angels, were later charged with involvement in the murder.

Forty Elephants.description: An all-female London crime syndicate active from the late 19th century to the 1950s, specializing in shoplifting. Based in the Elephant and Castle area, they raided West End stores and sometimes posed as housemaids to rob homes. The gang was allied with the Elephant and Castle Mob and avoided police detection for decades.

descriptions: Forty Thieves listed as ally of Dead Rabbits in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chichesters, Tammany Hall, Plug Uglies, Roach Guards, Mulberry Street Boys, Municipal Police, Forty Thieves, Shirt Tails, Kerryonians

descriptions: The Forty Elephants were allied with the Elephant and Castle Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Elephant and Castle Mob

descriptions: The infobox lists Forty Thieves as an ally of the Kerryonians.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Forty Thieves, Shirt Tails, Chichesters, Dead Rabbits, Tammany Hall

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Forty Thieves.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Forty Thieves is listed as an ally of Shirt Tails in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Forty Thieves, Roach Guards, Tammany Hall Chichesters, Dead Rabbits

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood uses FAIM for street operations.
edges.evidence_quote: The San Quentin State Aryan Brotherhood uses the gang Family Affiliated Irish Mafia (FAIM) for its street-operated activity.

descriptions: 118 East Coast Crips attacked Bounty Hunter Bloods, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the 118 East Coast Crips shot a school bus in the hopes of killing members of the Bounty Hunter Bloods

Rasta City.description: Rasta City is a gang based in Trinidad and Tobago, originally formed after a split from Muslim City. It is currently led by 'Prezzi' and is involved in ongoing conflicts with rival gangs including Sixx and ABG. The gang has been active in gangland violence, with its former leader Akini 'Dole' Adams killed in a police raid in 2019. A peace deal with Sixx in 2023 was short-lived.

descriptions: Rasta City and ABG are enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Their current enemies also include 6 and ABG (Anybody Gets It)

descriptions: Rasta City and Muslim City were rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Their main rival used to be Muslim City

descriptions: Rasta City and Sixx/6ixx/6 are currently enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Their current enemies also include 6

descriptions: Sixx/6ixx/6 split from Rasta City.
edges.evidence_quote: after a falling out between some Rasta City members, a split occurred, leading to the formation of Sixx/6ixx/6 and Seven, the latter of which is now called Rasta City

descriptions: Rasta City agreed to a peace deal with Sixx.
edges.evidence_quote: In November 2023 it was reported in the news that Rasta City had agreed to a peace deal with Sixx.

descriptions: Islamic Legion and Tajammu al-Arabi shared members and support.
edges.evidence_quote: The two organizations shared members and a source of support, and the distinction between them is often ambiguous.

Al-Nusra Front.description: Sunni Islamist political and paramilitary group that fought in the Syrian civil war to overthrow Bashar al-Assad and establish an Islamic state. Formed in 2012, it was the official Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda from 2013 to 2016. In 2016 it broke ties with Al-Qaeda and renamed itself Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham, then merged into Hayat Tahrir Al-Sham in 2017.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham and al-Nusra Front became rivalrous with clashes in 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: since late 2016, Al-Nusra and Ahrar al-Sham have been increasingly rivalrous, with military clashes between them taking place in the Idlib Governorate in January–March 2017 and July 2017.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham allied with al-Nusra Front before 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Before 2016, Ahrar al-Sham allied with the al-Nusra Front

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front is listed as an ally (sometimes) in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Al-Nusra Front / Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham ( sometimes )

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham was allied with al-Nusra Front before 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Before 2016, Ahrar al-Sham allied with the al-Nusra Front, a now-defunct affiliate of al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Jabhat Fatah al-Sham merged with Ahrar al-Sham's faction to form Tahrir al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: through a merger of Ahrar al-Sham's Jaysh al-Ahrar faction, Jabhat Fatah al-Sham, Nur al-Din Zenki and other militia groups

descriptions: Al-Nusra clashed with Ahrar Al-Sham in 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: following violent clashes with Ahrar Al-Sham and other rebel groups

descriptions: Ahrar Al-Sham formed operations room to combat Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar Al-Sham, the Suqour Al-Sham Brigade, Jabhat Ahl Al-Sham, Jaysh Al-Islam and Fastaqim Union then established a joint operations room to combat Al-Nusra and its subfaction Jund Al-Aqsa.

descriptions: Al-Nusra defended Ahrar al-Sham by threatening ISIL after the killing of its commander.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Julani threatened to go to war with ISIL over their suspected role in the killing of senior Ahrar Al-Sham commander Abu Khaled Al-Souri.

descriptions: JFS fought alongside Ahrar Al-Sham in Aleppo offensive.
edges.evidence_quote: The mid-2016 rebel offensive in Aleppo was carried out by Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham alongside the Turkistan Islamic Party and Ahrar Al-Sham.

descriptions: Ahrar ash-Sham supplied weapons to Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: Weapons have been passed on to Nusra by Ahrar ash-Sham according to a Nusra member and rebels.

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Ajnad al-Kavkaz.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ajnad al-Kavkaz

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Al-Abbas Brigade.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Al-Abbas Brigade

descriptions: Al-Nusra cooperated with Al-Fajr Islamic Movement in attacking a Syrian air defense base.
edges.evidence_quote: In the air defence base assault they reportedly destroyed buildings and sabotaged radar and rockets after over-running the base in co-operation with Al-Fajr Islamic Movement and a group of Chechen fighters.

descriptions: Al-Nusra broke ties with Al-Qaeda in 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2016, Al-Nusra announced that it was breaking ties with Al-Qaeda

descriptions: Al-Nusra was the Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda from 2013 to 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2013, Al-Nusra Front was publicly confirmed as the official Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda

descriptions: Al-Nusra pledged allegiance to Al-Qaeda, making it the official Al-Qaeda branch in Syria.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Julani rejected the proposed merger and affirmed the group's allegiance to Al-Qaeda and its leader, Ayman Al-Zawahiri.

descriptions: Al-Nusra was an official branch of Al-Qaeda until July 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Jabhat Al-Nusra was an official branch of Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: The U.S. considered Al-Nusra an alias of Al-Qaeda in Iraq, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: On 10 December 2012, the U.S. designated Nusra a foreign terrorist organization and an alias of Al-Qaeda in Iraq.

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Ansar al-Din Front.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ansar al-Din Front

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Ansar al-Islam.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ansar al-Islam

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Ashida'a Mujahideen Brigade.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ashida'a Mujahideen Brigade

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Caucasus Emirate.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Caucasus Emirate

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba

descriptions: Al-Nusra considered Hezbollah an enemy and fought them.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra Front leader Julani stated that 'We are only here to accomplish one mission, to fight the regime and its agents on the ground, including Hezbollah and others'

descriptions: Al-Nusra targeted Hezbollah leader with bounty.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra offered bounties worth millions of dollars for the killing of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad and Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah.

descriptions: Hezbollah attacked JFS outpost.
edges.evidence_quote: On 31 August, Hezbollah attacked a JFS outpost in the Qalamoun Mountains near the city of Arsal, Lebanon, on the Lebanon–Syria border, killing a number of their fighters.

descriptions: Al-Nusra loyalists formed Hurras Al-Din to fight HTS.
edges.evidence_quote: Sami Al-Oraydi, alongside other Al-Qaeda loyalists ... mobilised Al-Qaeda personnel in northwestern Syria to establish an anti-HTS front in the region, eventually forming Hurras Al-Din

descriptions: Al-Nusra absorbed Imam Bukhari Jamaat in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 2015 Nusra absorbed Imam Bukhari Jamaat, an Uzbek group which is a part of Al-Qaeda

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Islamic Muthanna Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic Muthanna Movement

descriptions: Al-Nusra and ISIL were rival jihadist groups.
edges.evidence_quote: The tactics of Al-Nusra Front differed markedly from those of its rival jihadist group ISIL

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed the Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic State

descriptions: Al-Nusra and ISIL engaged in open armed conflict after a dispute over merger.
edges.evidence_quote: Open fighting between al-Nusra and ISIL (2013–2015)

descriptions: ISIS and Al-Nusra conducted a joint operation to capture Lebanese soldiers and police.
edges.evidence_quote: a joint ISIS–al-Nusra operation along with 29 civilians, some of whom are known terrorists.

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Jaysh al-Islam until 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Jaysh al-Islam (until 2016)

descriptions: Jaysh Al-Islam fought alongside Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: Jaysh Al-Islam leader Zahran Alloush addressed Jabhat Al-Nusra as 'our brothers', saying 'We fight alongside them and they fight alongside us'.

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Jaysh al-Jihad.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Jaysh al-Jihad

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Jund al-Aqsa.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Jund al-Aqsa

descriptions: Jund Al-Aqsa fighters joined Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: On 17 February 2016, nearly half of Jund Al-Aqsa, numbering 400 fighters, joined Al-Nusra.

descriptions: Jund Al-Aqsa merged with Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: On 9 October, Jund Al-Aqsa, after infighting between them and numerous rebel groups including Ahrar Al-Sham, merged with JFS.

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Kata'ib Hezbollah.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Kata'ib Hezbollah

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada

descriptions: Khorasan Group coordinated with Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: Khorasan, also known as the Khorasan Group, is an alleged group of senior Al-Qaeda members who operate in Syria. The group has been reported to ... co-ordinate with Al-Nusra Front

descriptions: Khorasan Group trained Al-Nusra's elite sniper unit.
edges.evidence_quote: Training is conducted in Aleppo by veteran jihadists who belong to the Khorasan Group, a collection of veteran Al-Qaeda operatives sent from Al-Qaeda strongholds along the Afghan-Pakistan border.

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed the PKK.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... PKK

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Liwa Fatemiyoun.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Liwa Fatemiyoun

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Liwa Zainebiyoun.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Liwa Zainebiyoun

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Malhama Tactical.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Malhama Tactical

descriptions: Al-Nusra and Al-Zenki jointly operated a training camp.
edges.evidence_quote: The training camp had been operated by Al-Nusra Front and Al-Zenki since 2013.

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed the YPG.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... YPG

descriptions: After the merger, Al-Nusra loyalists clashed with HTS.
edges.evidence_quote: Mutual hostility progressively deteriorated into violent confrontations, with Al-Nusra commander Sami al-Oraydi accusing HTS of adopting nationalist doctrines

descriptions: JFS merged into HTS in 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham (JFS) merged with four other groups to form Hayat Tahrir Al-Sham (HTS)

descriptions: Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham merged into Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: On 28 January 2017, several Salafi jihadist groups, including Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham, agreed upon a merger agreement, resulting in the formation of a new group called Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham.

descriptions: Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham is the successor organization to Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham wounded militants were receiving treatment in Israel.

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Turkistan Islamic Party.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Turkistan Islamic Party

descriptions: Turkistan Islamic Party offered condolences to JFS, indicating alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Condolences were issued to Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham over the killings, by the Uyghur jihadist Turkistan Islamic Party.

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed the YPJ.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... YPJ

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Yarmouk Martyrs Brigade.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Yarmouk Martyrs Brigade

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra Front (dissolved in 2017, merged with other Islamist organizations to form Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham and split ties)

descriptions: Jabhat Al-Nusra was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Qatar financed al-Qaeda's enterprises through al-Qaeda's former affiliate in Syria, Jabhat Al-Nusra.

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front is linked to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: al-Qaeda linked Al-Nusra Front

descriptions: AQAP and al-Nusra Front were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies al-Nusra Front (2012–2017)

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front claimed responsibility for an attack that killed Hezbollah fighters.
edges.evidence_quote: On 18 January 2015, a group of Hezbollah fighters was targeted in Quneitra, with the Al-Nusra Front claiming responsibility.

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front is listed as an opponent of Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Al-Nusra Front

descriptions: Al-Nusra and ISIL are hostile towards each other, with differing approaches to Druze.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra and ISIL are both hostile towards the Druze. However, while al-Nusra has typically destroyed Druze shrines and pressured them to convert to Sunni Islam, ISIL regards the entire Druze community as a valid target for violence, as it does the Yazidis.

descriptions: ISIL and al-Nusra Front occasionally cooperate against the Syrian government.
edges.evidence_quote: ISIL and al-Nusra Front still cooperate with each other occasionally when they fight against the Syrian government.

descriptions: Early fighters from the Khorasan region were sent to join al-Nusra Front in Syria before ISIS–K was formed.
edges.evidence_quote: Hafiz Saeed Khan ... agreed to rapidly assemble a group of 143 Afghan and Pakistani volunteer fighters for Al-Qaeda to dispatch them to join the al-Nusra Front in Syria.

descriptions: TTP sent fighters to Syria to fight alongside Al-Nusra Front.
edges.evidence_quote: the TTP's media wing ... claimed to have ... sent hundreds of fighters to Syria to fight alongside ... the Al-Nusra Front

descriptions: Infobox lists Al-Nusra Front as an opponent of Wagner Group from 2014 to 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: List: Al-Nusra Front (2014–2017)

descriptions: The Bellocco were in conflict with the Amato family, with a possible war prevented by arrests.
edges.evidence_quote: per prevenire una possibile guerra a Bologna con l'emergente famiglia di etnia rom degli Amato

descriptions: Mezcales were formerly CJNG enforcers but later became independent.
edges.evidence_quote: Mezcales, which previously acted as the CJNG's local enforcers, were no longer affiliated with CJNG.

descriptions: Mezcales acted as local enforcers for CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Mezcales, which up until this report of El Mencho's death, acted as the CJNG's local enforcers.

descriptions: Kishimoto-gumi is implied to be a Yamaguchi-gumi clan as Kishimoto was a high-ranking Yamaguchi-gumi member.
edges.evidence_quote: He was also the godfather or kumichō of the Kishimoto-gumi

descriptions: The Petullà (Ieraci-Petullà) and Squillace cooperated in drug trafficking, as shown by the operation Alba e tramonto.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 1º luglio 2008 termina l'operazione Alba e tramonto della polizia di Reggio Calabria e Polistena con 27 arresti di affiliati alle cosche Ieraci-Petullà di Cinquefrondi e Squillace di Polistena in collaborazione anche con appartenenti ai Nirta-Strangio.

descriptions: The Squillace and Nirta-Strangio cooperated in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 1º luglio 2008 termina l'operazione Alba e tramonto della polizia di Reggio Calabria e Polistena con 27 arresti di affiliati alle cosche Ieraci-Petullà di Cinquefrondi e Squillace di Polistena in collaborazione anche con appartenenti ai Nirta-Strangio.

descriptions: Collaboration with Squillace in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 1º luglio 2008 termina l'operazione Alba e tramonto ... con 27 arresti di affiliati ... Squillace di Polistena in collaborazione anche con appartenenti ai Nirta-Strangio.

descriptions: FLB-ARB is listed as an ally of ETA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Batasuna , Herri Batasuna , FARC , FLB-ARB , Provisional IRA , Terra Lliure

The Commission.description: The governing body of the American Mafia, formed in 1931 by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano after the Castellammarese War. It consists of the bosses of the Five Families of New York City, the Chicago Outfit, and occasionally other families. The Commission oversees Mafia activities in the United States and mediates conflicts between families. It was involved in the Apalachin meeting, a 1963 murder plot, and the 1985 Mafia Commission Trial.

descriptions: Ugly Americans is a smaller unit within The Americans.
edges.evidence_quote: The Americans gang is made up of smaller units such as the Ugly Americans, Young Americans, Spoilt Brats, Dollar Boys, Dollar Kids, Young Dixie Boys and Sexy Boys.

descriptions: George Street had a bitter rivalry with Taylor's Alley.
edges.evidence_quote: a bitter rivalry with the neighbouring Taylor's Alley (Bloods) which bled into the 2010s

descriptions: Mala del Brenta had business relationships with the Fidanzati mafia group in Milan.
edges.evidence_quote: Oltre ai legami con il gruppo mafioso facente capo ai Fidanzati di Milano e a Salvatore Enea, venivano accertati frequenti “rapporti d'affari” con esponenti della Camorra...

descriptions: Cosa Nostra clans Fidanzati and Emmanuello obtained drugs from 'Ndrangheta in 2009.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2009 i siciliani si rivolgono nuovamente alle 'ndrine per rifornirsi di droga, i Fidanzati di Palermo e gli Emmanuello di Gela prendevano la droga colombiana spedita attraverso i porti di Anversa e Amburgo.

descriptions: Gallico, Licciardi, and Filippone were arrested together for drug trafficking, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 21 marzo 2018 vengono effettuati 19 arresti a Roma di presunti elementi appartenenti al clan Licciardi della Camorra e ai Filippone e Gallico accusati di traffico di droga

descriptions: The Rinaldi clan had a pact on the drugs business with the Piezzo group.
edges.evidence_quote: De Bernardo was killed for violating the pact on the drugs business with the leaders of the Piezzo group, but above all, with the boss Ciro Rinaldi.

descriptions: Sons of Silence members attacked a Bad 7 member, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Sons of Silence members... pleaded guilty to charges stemming from an incident at a bar in Rock Springs in which they beat and robbed Bradley Chrisman, a member of the rival Bad 7 motorcycle club, of his colors on April 10, 2017.

descriptions: Atomwaffen members sought training with Azov Battalion.
edges.evidence_quote: Atomwaffen members also sought to train with the Azov Battalion and Russian Imperial Movement.

descriptions: Atomwaffen members attended Azov training camps.
edges.evidence_quote: According to an investigation by German Amadeu Antonio Foundation published on April 19, 2021, the members were acquiring Uzi submachine guns and had attended Azov training camps in Ukraine.

descriptions: AFCA Youth is a main group within AFCA.
edges.evidence_quote: The main groups within the AFCA hooligan firm are: - AFCA Youth

descriptions: Latin Pachucos now control former Jousters territory, implying conflict or succession.
edges.evidence_quote: The Latin Pachucos now control Hanson Park

descriptions: Serpa and Martello were in conflict in the Paola feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Paola: faida in atto tra il clan Serpa contro i Martello per il controllo del territorio di Paola.

descriptions: Bamboo Union cooperated with Iron Blood Patriots for overseas missions and heroin trade.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1980s, the gang became even more powerful when they joined hands with the "Iron Blood Patriots" to carry out missions overseas.
